Off Calendar Jul-08-2009 Continued To Aug-12-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This case was called with case number 246349 (Estate of P.C. Tam).

Appearances: J. Virginia Peiser and Vivian Lynn Holley for Diana Tom. Max Gutierrez and Natacha Ivacheff-Kolb for the Trustees.

Ms. Holly stated that they have had two four-way meetings, that they just received a new property management agreement and she would like to confer with Counsel, that they would like to mediate, that the accountings from mid-2006 to 2009 were submitted, that earlier accountings won't be necessary, that they need to narrow down how much money was in the Trust and who it is to be distributed to, that the only issue with Powell Street is six months rent, and that no repairs were done on the Pacific Avenue property by the Mother. Mr. Gutierrez stated that his clients are incurring a lot of expense, that the accounting for 2003-2006 filed in May does not conform to Probate Code 1060 or the Rules of Court, that neither accounting balances, that money is owed to Ms. Tom for what she paid out, that the place is in abominable condition, and that 3 units haven't been rented for 3 1/2 years; all under the tenure of Ms. Diana Tom. In addition, he stated that the Court removed Diana Tom as Trustee, that there was a total lack of fiduciary duties, and that he was requesting Diana Tom not interfere with the the management of the building. The Court stated that this Estate is too large for Pro Bono mediation, ordered a meet and confer regarding the management of the property/transfer of funds held by Diana Tom by July 31, 2009, and that the parties are to select a date by July 10th or the Court would. All matters were continued to 8-12-09 and a report is to be sent to the Court prior to 8-12. The Court noted that if it is to address the accountings then the objections should be filed. Mr. Gutierrez said if they don't settle he will file written objections.

Judge: David Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

Off Calendar Mar-10-2009 Continued To Jun-03-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 246349 (Estate of P. C. Tam).

Appearances: Vivian Holley and Virginia Peiser for Diana Tom. Max Gutierrez and Natacha Ivacheff-Kolb for Cathy Tom and for Jackie Tom.

Ms. Peiser stated that they were new Counsel and that they were prepared to comply with the Court's order. In addition, she stated that she was planning to file the accounting today and that she would turn over the tax returns with the appraisals attached to Mr. Gutierrez. The Parties raised argument as to the status on the Powell Street property, the Pacific Street property, multiple deeds on the properties, fees and the accounting. The matters were continued to June 3, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to allow new Counsel time to sort things out.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Lee.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Distribution Of The Powell Street Property, All Income And Assets Derived Therefrom
Off Calendar Mar-10-2009 Continued To Jun-03-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
